---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
##library(readxl)
##library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size70[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size45[.center[
Evaluate Universal Jurisdiction (UJ) as a strategy for reducing government violence against its citizens
]]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

### Prep for Class
1. ...

<br>



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-green_blue_swirl_side.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center

.size60[**Paper 2 Prompt**]

<br>

.size50[Propose a specific strategy for reducing political violence by state governments. This can be focused at any level of action (e.g. individual, group, states, etc.).]

???

This is an argument paper so every claim must be supported by evidence!

You can start from any of the examples we've discussed in class over the last two weeks or one of the two ideas we'll be studying this week.

### Any questions on the assignment?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/09_1-UJ.jpg')
background-size: 90%
background-position: center

???

Today we'll explore the global norm of universal jurisdiction (UJ) as a "strategy for reducing government violence against their citizens"



---

class: middle, slideblue

.size60[.center[**Universal Jurisdiction**]]

.size50[
+ Therefore, the US should oppose efforts to establish universal jurisdiction as an international norm.

+ Therefore, the US should work to advance the establishment of universal jurisdiction as an international norm.
]

???

We need to split the class into two groups to cover the two sides of this argument.

### So, who wants to work on which side of the debate?

*Split class into two groups*

- Even them out if groups very uneven

<br>

Please keep in mind, the goal today is NOT TO WIN the argument.

The goal is to make sure we fully understand the trade-offs in embracing or rejecting this policy option.

In 10 minutes I want each group to put their strongest case leading to this conclusion on the board.

- Don't stress about this being perfect in its first draft, we're going to get feedback and go back to revise.

<br>

*Present Each*

Ok, groups take 5 minutes to prep feedback / counter-argument for the other group.

Remember, the goal here is not to win, it is to make sure each argument fully engages with the trade-offs in embracing or rejecting UJ.

<br>

*Share Feedback*

Groups, take the feedback on board and now work on revising your argument on the board.

<br>

*Present Each*

<br>

Ok class, where do we end up?

### Under what specific conditions, do the trade-offs tip towards supporting the norm?

### Under what specific conditions, do the trade-offs tip away from  supporting the norm?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-green_blue_swirl_side.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center

.size60[**Paper 2 Prompt**]

<br>

.size50[Propose a specific strategy for reducing political violence by state governments. This can be focused at any level of action (e.g. individual, group, states, etc.).]

???

### Is anybody leaning towards writing a paper endorsing UJ? Why or why not?
